title: Negotiations for the Northaven Charter
content:
The Northaven Charter (1990) was a diplomatic agreement in which Rhea Jordan, army liaison of Republic of Dorsaly, helped finalize terms at Glimmerford.

Primary sources.

Archived minutes from the Glimmerford conference hall include Rhea Jordan's opening address and the ratification roll call.

Background.

By the early 1990s, diplomats from Republic of Dorsaly sought a settlement after years of border incidents near Glimmerford.

Implementation.

Commission reports from 1991 show gradual demobilization and the reopening of overland routes through the Glimmerford basin.

Reactions.

Newspapers in Republic of Dorsaly praised the compromise, while opposition leaders argued that naval access points near Glimmerford were not sufficiently protected.

Signing and terms.

The Northaven Charter was signed in Glimmerford on 14 September 1990. The agreement established trade corridors and a joint commission chaired initially by Rhea Jordan.

Historical assessment.

Modern historians view the Northaven Charter as a stabilizing milestone, crediting Rhea Jordan's insistence on verifiable inspection clauses.

Negotiations.

Rhea Jordan, serving as army liaison, chaired preliminary talks that began in spring 1989. Neutral observers from neighboring cantons attended the sessions.

Primary sources.

Archived minutes from the Glimmerford conference hall include Rhea Jordan's opening address and the ratification roll call.
